name: Fook Yuen Seafood Restaurant
tagline: Legit dim sum with rolling carts — the har gow and char siu bao are Chinatown quality without the Chinatown parking.
mustOrder: Har gow — delicate shrimp dumplings with translucent wrappers
Char siu bao — fluffy BBQ pork buns, steamed to order
Siu mai — pork and shrimp dumplings, a dim sum staple
parking: Parking lot in the McCully Shopping Center. Usually can find a spot.
talkStory: Fook Yuen is where Honolulu's Chinese aunties and uncles go for weekend dim sum — the ultimate credibility marker. They still do traditional rolling cart service: flag down the cart ladies and point at what looks good. Operating since the early 1990s in McCully Shopping Center, it's one of the few remaining cart-style dim sum spots on Oahu. Weekend dim sum packs out by 10am; weekday dim sum is the local hack — same food, zero wait. Dinner seafood is solid but dim sum is the event.
